Relatable if in the maintenance/ operations department:
1. No work-life balance at all. (Have to be involved with the shopfloor activities 24x7)
2. Obsolete technology, and no learning to prepare for the future
3. Lots of politics (very stressful and frustrating environment maintained)
4. Most locations are remote at TSL
5. No software used, I only used Excel for 2 1/2 years.
6. Senior management is extremely poor and unprofessional. Most of them make others do all their work and even write emails for them.

Personal experience:
I joined TSL from campus placement as a management trainee and then transitioned into Manager mechanical maintenance after 1 year. After almost 3 years of work experience, I have not learned much at all, and I am completely unprepared to work with any progressive technology. The entire industry is obsolete and to make matters worse, there is a lot of politics and unprofessionalism in the organization. Many more negatives, but in short I will never recommend anyone to start their career in this organization.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Tata Steel offers numerous benefits for employees, including competitive salaries, comprehensive health benefits, and robust retirement plans. The company emphasizes professional growth through training programs, career development opportunities, and global exposure. Employees enjoy a supportive work environment that values diversity and inclusivity. Tata Steel's strong commitment to corporate social responsibility fosters a sense of pride and purpose. The company also offers various wellness programs, ensuring a healthy work-life balance. With its focus on innovation and sustainability, Tata Steel provides a dynamic workplace where employees can contribute to impactful projects and be part of an industry leader.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: Working at Tata Steel can have its drawbacks. Employees may face challenges such as long working hours and high-pressure environments, especially during project deadlines. There can be limited work-life balance due to demanding schedules. Additionally, career progression might be slow, with rigid hierarchical structures sometimes hindering swift promotions. The large organizational size can lead to bureaucratic red tape, making decision-making processes slower. Lastly, despite efforts to modernize, some traditional practices may persist, affecting the agility and adaptability of employees in a rapidly changing industry.

review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: Working as a Manager at Tata Steel's Kalinganagar plant can offer several advantages and disadvantages.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Established and reputable company: Tata Steel is one of the largest steel producers in India and has a strong reputation globally. Working for such a renowned company can enhance your professional credentials and provide opportunities for growth. Career advancement opportunities: Tata Steel offers a structured career progression path and invests in employee development and training programs. You may have access to various learning and skill-building opportunities that can help you advance your career within the organization. Competitive compensation and benefits: Tata Steel provides competitive compensation packages, including salary, bonuses, and benefits such as healthcare, insurance, retirement plans, and employee welfare programs. This can contribute to financial security and job satisfaction. Challenging and diverse work environment: The steel industry is dynamic and fast-paced. Working at the Kalinganagar plant can expose you to a wide range of responsibilities, projects, and challenges. This can enhance your skills, knowledge, and problem-solving abilities. Commitment to sustainability and social responsibility: Tata Steel is known for its focus on sustainability, community development, and corporate social responsibility initiatives. Contributing to such efforts can provide a sense of purpose and pride in your work.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: Demanding work environment: The steel industry can be physically demanding and involves working in industrial settings. Depending on your role, you may face long working hours, tight deadlines, and occasional exposure to hazards. Operational challenges: Managing a plant requires dealing with complex operational issues, including production targets, maintenance requirements, supply chain management, and adherence to safety protocols. Handling these challenges can be demanding and require strong leadership and problem-solving skills. Relocation requirements: If you are not already located in Kalinganagar or the vicinity, working at the Tata Steel plant may involve relocating to the area. Relocation can disrupt personal and family life, requiring adjustments and potentially uprooting from familiar surroundings. Bureaucratic processes: Large organizations like Tata Steel may have bureaucratic processes and decision-making structures that can slow down decision-making and hinder agility. This can sometimes create frustration and delays in implementing ideas or projects. review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: A company you can fall in love with.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
( Only for TSL , Jamshedpur ) 
1) Extremely outstanding culture. The people who works on the floor respects and loves the company.
2) Pro employee policies , especially towards the people who make things happen on the floor.
3) Well knit group , teams develop good bonding over time and share more than good professional relations ( also due to the culture / size and other factors of Jamshedpur / Jharkhand ) 
4) Access to leadership is very easy and despite having a very strict hierarchical structure the communication are two way.
5) Extremely mature and extraordinary union, likes of which you will hardly find anywhere in India.
6) Work life balance , lovely city , lots of facility and a very good club.
7) A great learning hub and lots of stalwarts of the field.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
1) Needs to be more agile 
2) Promote meritocracy 
3) Due to size and structure also excellent systems and mechanisms exists , proper review and adherence is poor.
4) Unstructured talent development strategy. Recent  leadership changes have led to some loss of faith. 
5) Legacy vs Change 
6) Slow pace of life can be unsettling for starters 
7) Office politics plays major role in almost all decision making processes

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Job comfort means job security, and that can lead to less anxiety. Without the constant worry and stress of losing your job or trying to over-perform to keep your job, you live and work your day-to-day with relative ease. You can voice your opinion without fear of repercussions. You can put away for your retirement or send Jane to private school. In an era when a good many people are unemployed, any sense of job comfort can instill a deep sense of gratitude, fortune and luck.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: Job comfort can lead you to become – and let’s not sugar coat this – bored. You begin to feel you’re not living up to your full potential, and without new challenges you lose confidence in yourself. Here's how Lund University researcher Roland Paulsen explains it in a recent article in The Atlantic: “Involuntary slacking may first be conceived of as real bliss: ‘Hey, I don’t have to work!’ ... But as the years pass by, most of us will crave some type of meaningful activity.”

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
- Great pay and benefits. 
- Huge company, great for learning the ins and outs of an integrated steel plant. 
- There is very little toxicity in the upper level management, they are great people as long as you get work done. 
- Regard to safety is impressive, given that in Indian regard to safety is disgraceful. TATA implies effective solutions to ensuring safety. And they do ensure it.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
- Somewhat socialistic. Salary at engineer level is only slightly more than salary at lower levels. This is not the problem but it leads to problems such as people not being motivated to do anything extraordinary. 
- Departments are huge and aiming for upper level management will have to involve more than just doing good work. You'll have to be in the books of the bosses, make an effort to reach out to them. Sometimes, that is tougher than simply inviting them for coffee or lunch. Bigger the company, more convoluted are the bureaucracies.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Great team camaraderie at lower grades
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Little to no career progression prospects for those working at the lower graded roles.

Graduates attain preferential treatment.

No discussion or exploration of potential training or up-skilling unless it directly involves operating, serves or benefits a manufacturing unit.

Manning levels tend to run extremely lean. 

Staff are expected to pick up workload for any sickness or retirements due to company's inability to hire new staff in timely manner. Production output expectations are not revised to reflect this and stress the remaining staff despite numerous mental health e-learning modules being rolled out to upper and local management.

Safety initiatives are often re-active and punitive rather than pro-active and engaging.

"Who you know" hiring practices doesn't deliver the best possible additions to the workforce.

No regular 1-2-1s with team supervisor / local management team
